alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes a specific reduction (worst-case probability to discounted reward) that operationalizes how model-free RL is applied; this clarifies the original modeling statement though it does not contradict it.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The lazy color generation method and approximate method proposed in the study address scalability issues when the number of colors in the acceptance condition of the DPA is large.

alignments.rationale: The publication expresses the same contribution (lazy color generation and approximation) addressing scalability, with only slight wording changes (colors vs sets).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The approach significantly outperforms existing methods for learning controllers from LTL specifications in stochastic games.

alignments.rationale: The publication qualifies the outperforming result by specifying it holds in several case studies, which narrows the scope from an overall claim to observed case-study evidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Most hyperparameter optimization methods have not been explored in terms of their efficiencies on small datasets in the molecular domain.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same limitation claim about exploration of HPO methods on small molecular datasets.

alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the contribution claim about the theoretical analysis with no substantive changes.

alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the implementation and adoption claim without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our method is fair as it assigns the desirable outcome to the most qualified individuals and removes the effect of stereotypes in decision-making.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method outperforms traditional classification algorithms in terms of fairness and effectiveness.

alignments.rationale: The core claim is preserved, but the publication lists a lower certainty level (medium vs. high), so confidence is tempered.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The GDC is conducive to convolutional neural networks to form an overall impression of the image. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The GDC is conducive to convolutional neural networks forming an overall impression of the image. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Wording is slightly adjusted but the conclusion and expressed confidence remain the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed GDC can be easily used as a module to build lightweight or complex segmentation networks. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: No corresponding publication claim asserts ease of modular integration for lightweight or complex networks; this contribution appears omitted in the published version.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The extension of the MAP-Elites algorithm by treating parent selection as a multi-armed bandit problem can accelerate the discovery of new regions in the search space.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The paper extends the MAP-Elites algorithm by treating parent selection as a multi-armed bandit problem.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the methodological contribution but splits the implementation claim from outcome statements, so the core contribution is clarified (stated as the extension) rather than repeating the specific acceleration outcome in the same sentence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using variations of the upper-confidence bound for parent selection improves the total quality of the archive.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using variations of the upper-confidence bound for parent selection can accelerate the discovery of new regions in the search space.
alignments.rationale: The publication changes the claimed effect from archive quality improvement to accelerating discovery of new regions: the focus of the claim is revised to a different outcome (discovery vs. quality).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Maintaining a balance between exploration and exploitation leads to the most diverse and high-quality set of solutions in three different testbeds.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Maintaining a balance between exploration and exploitation leads to the most diverse and high-quality set of solutions.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same result claim (balance yields diverse, high-quality solutions); only the explicit mention of the three testbeds is omitted but the claim remains semantically the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: ARCH-Elites is a MAP-Elites implementation that can reconfigure large-scale urban layouts at real-world locations using a pre-trained surrogate model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: ARCH-Elites is a MAP-Elites implementation that can reconfigure large-scale urban layouts using a pre-trained surrogate model.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same implementation contribution; the slight wording change (omitting "at real-world locations") does not change the claim's meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method generates novel urban designs for two real-world locations in Boston, Massachusetts.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method generates novel urban designs for two real-world locations in Boston, Massachusetts.
alignments.rationale: Identical result claim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Combining the exploration of a possibility space with real-time performance evaluation creates a new paradigm for architectural generative design.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Combining exploration of a possibility space with real-time performance evaluation creates a new paradigm for architectural generative design.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conceptual conclusion without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Incorporating virtual adversarial training (VAT) into attention mechanisms allows for the computation of adversarial perturbations from unlabeled data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Incorporating virtual adversarial training (VAT) into attention mechanisms allows for the computation of adversarial perturbations from unlabeled data.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same methodological contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Attention iVAT can efficiently learn clearer attention and improve model interpretability, even with unlabeled data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Attention iVAT can efficiently learn clearer attention and improve model interpretability, even with unlabeled data.
alignments.rationale: The result claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Empirical experiments demonstrate that the proposed techniques provide better prediction performance than conventional adversarial training-based and VAT-based techniques.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Empirical experiments revealed that the proposed techniques provide better prediction performance than conventional adversarial training-based and VAT-based techniques.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same empirical finding with only minor wording change (demonstrate -> revealed).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The VAT-based technique improves both prediction performance and model interpretability without requiring careful selection of unlabeled data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The VAT-based technique improves prediction performance and model interpretability without the need for careful selection of unlabeled data.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the conclusion that the VAT-based approach improves performance and interpretability and relaxes unlabeled data selection requirements.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel deep learning-based weighted interpolation estimator is proposed to accurately estimate vehicular channels in high mobility scenarios.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A novel deep learning-based weighted interpolation estimator is proposed to accurately estimate vehicular channels in high mobility scenarios.
alignments.rationale: The core methodological contribution is restated identically in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed estimator modifies the pilot allocation of the IEEE 802.11p standard to achieve higher transmission data rates.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed estimator modifies the pilot allocation of the IEEE 802.11p standard to achieve more transmission data rates.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same idea but slightly rephrases the expected effect on transmission rates; this is a minor clarification rather than a substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The developed estimator significantly outperforms recently proposed DL-based frame-by-frame estimators in various vehicular scenarios.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The developed estimator significantly outperforms recently proposed DL-based frame-by-frame estimators in different vehicular scenarios.
alignments.rationale: The publication asserts the same comparative performance claim with only trivial wording variation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed estimator substantially reduces the overall computational complexity compared to existing methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed estimator substantially reduces the overall computational complexity compared to existing methods.
alignments.rationale: The computational-complexity reduction claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method can protect the copyright of DNN models effectively.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method has better performance on watermark accuracy and robustness against the query modification attack compared with three existing DNN watermarking methods.
alignments.rationale: The publication replaces a broad effectiveness claim with more specific comparative performance evidence; the general notion of effective protection is therefore clarified by concrete results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method can obtain 100% watermark accuracy and 100% fingerprint authentication success rate on Fashion-MNIST and CIFAR-10 datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method can obtain 100% watermark accuracy and 100% fingerprint authentication success rate on Fashion-MNIST and CIFAR-10 datasets.
alignments.rationale: Exact match between preprint and publication on reported dataset-level results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method is demonstrated to be robust to the model fine-tuning attack, model pruning attack, and the query modification attack.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method is demonstrated to be robust against the model fine-tuning attack, model pruning attack, and query modification attack.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the robustness demonstration verbatim (minor wording differences only).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method has better performance on watermark accuracy and robustness against the query modification attack compared with three existing watermarking methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method has better performance on watermark accuracy and robustness against the query modification attack compared with three existing DNN watermarking methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study proposes a method for the visual analytics of set data that supports knowledge discovery and member selection.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study proposes a method for the visual analytics of set data that supports knowledge discovery and member selection.
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication statements are essentially identical in content and emphasis, asserting the same contribution.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method utilizes a manifold network model to model the generation process of sets and their outputs.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method utilizes a manifold network model to model the generation process of sets and their outputs.
alignments.rationale: Both versions describe the same modeling approach with no substantive change in meaning or scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method visualizes relevant factors as a set of topographic maps, allowing users to interactively indicate their targets of interest.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method visualizes relevant factors as a set of topographic maps, allowing users to interactively indicate their targets of interest.
alignments.rationale: The visualization description is carried over verbatim and retains the same implications for interactivity.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method was demonstrated by applying it to basketball teams and compared with a benchmark system for outcome prediction and lineup reconstruction tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method was demonstrated by applying it to basketball teams and compared with a benchmark system for outcome prediction and lineup reconstruction tasks.
alignments.rationale: The demonstration and comparison claims are the same in both versions, with matching scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method can be adapted to individual application cases by extending the network structure, making it a general method for building practical systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method can be adapted to individual application cases by extending the network structure, making it a general method for building practical systems.
alignments.rationale: The conclusion about adaptability and generality is unchanged between preprint and final publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed probabilistic framework allows for the estimation of parameters of an acquisition function based on observed human behavior.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our method can recover a latent acquisition function used to generate θ2 conditional on randomly generated values of Δr1.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Many subjects exhibit exploration preferences that exceed those predicted by standard acquisition functions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Many subjects tend to exhibit exploration preferences beyond that of standard acquisition functions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Augmenting candidate acquisition functions based on model discrepancies leads to a superior fit to human behavior in the optimization task.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We augment the candidate acquisition functions to yield a superior fit to the human behavior in this task.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Molecular changes in tumors cause morphological changes in tissue that can be observed on the microscopic level.
alignments.rationale: This causal, general statement about molecular changes causing morphological changes does not appear among the publication's listed claims, which focus on the CNN analysis and results.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Out of 15586 protein coding transcripts, 6618 had predicted expression significantly associated with RNA-seq estimates in a cross-validation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Out of 15,586 protein coding transcripts, 6,618 had predicted expression significantly associated with RNA-seq estimates.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Contemporary computer vision models offer an inexpensive and scalable solution for prediction of gene expression phenotypes directly from WSIs.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed generative model can tune specific features while learning structural features of a graph from data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed generative model can tune specific features while learning structural features of a graph from data.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Three classical regression methods are able to achieve meaningful results for ELA-based algorithm selection. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The quality of the regression models is highly impacted by the chosen hyper-parameters. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Guidelines for the tuning of the regression models and general recommendations for a more systematic use of classical machine learning models in landscape-aware algorithm selection are derived. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The choice of the machine learning model merits careful consideration and further investigation. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The choice of the machine learning model merits careful consideration and further investigation. (certainty=medium, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: Wording and certainty are consistent between preprint and publication, so the claim is unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Autoencoders have been introduced in high energy physics as a promising tool for model-independent new physics searches. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Autoencoders have been introduced in high energy physics as a promising tool for model-independent new physics searches. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Recent results show excellent classification performance with synthetic datasets, approaching 100 percent performance across key evaluation metrics. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The three synthetic datasets are relatively similar within their group. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed LATTICE model includes a novel modality-aware structure learning layer that learns item-item structures for each modality. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed LATTICE model includes a novel modality-aware structure learning layer that learns item-item structures for each modality. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed TransCrowd can effectively extract the semantic crowd information by using the self-attention mechanism of transformer. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The classical convolution and recurrent structures in fault diagnosis models have defects in computational efficiency and feature representation. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The fuzzy pairwise constraint allows a supervisor to provide the grade of similarity or dissimilarity between the implicit fuzzy vectors of a pair of samples.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The diagonal block coordinate descent (DBCD) algorithm guarantees stationary points and can obtain global solutions under certain conditions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental results demonstrate the outperformance of the FDC compared with some state-of-the-art clustering models.

alignments.rationale: The publication emphasizes the optimization step (from initial mesh to isotropic mesh preserving features), which implicitly subsumes the preprint's claim that an initial mesh can be obtained; wording is more focused on optimization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The initial reconstructed mesh is optimized into an isotropic one with important geometric features.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The initial reconstructed mesh can be optimized into an isotropic one while preserving important geometric features.
alignments.rationale: The statement about optimizing the initial mesh into an isotropic one while preserving features is directly carried over.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The experimental results indicate that the proposed framework shows great advantages over peer ones in terms of mesh quality, geometric feature keeping, and processing speed.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental results indicate that the proposed framework shows great advantages over peer methods in terms of mesh quality, geometric feature keeping, and processing speed.
alignments.rationale: The reported experimental advantages over peer methods are maintained with the same scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Jacobian regularization greatly increases model robustness to Universal Adversarial Perturbations (UAPs) by up to four times whilst maintaining clean performance.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Jacobian regularization greatly increases model robustness to Universal Adversarial Perturbations (UAPs) by up to four times whilst maintaining clean performance.
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication statements are verbatim and convey the same result and certainty.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our theoretical analysis allows us to formulate a metric for the strength of shared adversarial perturbations between pairs of inputs.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same theoretical-contribution claim with equivalent meaning and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed metric is highly correlated with the actual observed robustness on benchmark datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The metric for shared adversarial perturbations is highly correlated with the actual observed robustness.
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert the same empirical correlation; wording is effectively identical.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Realistic and practical universal attacks can be reliably mitigated without sacrificing clean accuracy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Realistic and practical universal attacks can be reliably mitigated without sacrificing clean accuracy.
alignments.rationale: Conclusion matches exactly between preprint and publication in content and confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed transformer-based model effectively combines a reconstruction-based approach and patch embedding for image anomaly detection and localization.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The model processes embedded patches using a Gaussian mixture density network to localize anomalous areas.
alignments.rationale: The publication's claim set does not mention the Gaussian mixture density network; the specific architectural claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Mining health data can lead to faster medical decisions, improvement in the quality of treatment, disease prevention, reduced cost, and it drives innovative solutions within the healthcare sector.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Mining health data can lead to faster medical decisions, improvement in the quality of treatment, disease prevention, and reduced cost.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same high-level benefits of mining health data, omitting a phrase about driving innovation but keeping the substantive claims.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Anonymization or removal of patient identifiable information is the first important step to adhere to regulations and incorporate privacy concerns.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Anonymization or removal of patient identifiable information is the first important step to adhere to regulations and incorporate privacy concerns.
alignments.rationale: Identical regulatory/privacy recommendation present in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Anonymization, though necessary, is not sufficient to address patient privacy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Anonymization, although necessary, is not sufficient to address patient privacy.
alignments.rationale: Same cautionary conclusion carried over to the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We provide an overview of possible attacks on anonymized data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We provide an overview of possible attacks on anonymized data.
alignments.rationale: Overview-of-attacks contribution is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We discuss tools that can be used to achieve anonymization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We discuss tools that can be used to achieve anonymization.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains this contribution on anonymization tools.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed framework significantly improves over state-of-the-art dynamic optimisation algorithms.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed framework significantly improves over state-of-the-art dynamic optimization algorithms.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the main performance claim of significant improvement.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A mathematical formulation of dynamic CARP (DCARP) is provided in this paper.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A mathematical formulation of dynamic CARP (DCARP) is provided in this article.
alignments.rationale: The formalization/contribution is retained with only wording adaptation (paper->article).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework can utilize either a simple restart strategy or a sequence transfer strategy in response to dynamic events.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework can utilize either a simple restart strategy or a sequence transfer strategy in response to dynamic events.
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the same description of the adaptive strategies available in the framework.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The simulation system designed in this study can consider dynamic events while a routing solution is already partially executed.
alignments.rationale: No matching publication claim describing the simulation system handling partially-executed routing solutions is present; this specific simulation-system claim appears omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Voronoi fundamental zone octonion interpolation framework significantly reduces computation runtime for grain boundary structure-property models compared to traditional methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Voronoi fundamental zone (VFZ) framework offers an advantage over other 5DOF based property interpolation methods by significantly reducing runtime for computing Euclidean distances.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies that the runtime reduction advantage pertains to computing Euclidean distances in the VFZ (a more specific aspect) rather than the broader claim about full structure-property model runtime.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Grain boundary energy interpolation using Gaussian process regression achieved a reduction of the root mean square error by 83.0% relative to a constant average model.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Gaussian process regression (GPR) achieves the best performance in boundary energy interpolation, reducing the root mean square error (RMSE) by 83% compared to a constant average model.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the quantitative RMSE reduction for GPR matching the preprint result.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Interpolation on a large, noisy molecular statics Fe simulation dataset improves performance by 34.4% compared to 21.2% in prior work.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The GPR method demonstrates better performance in interpolating simulated bi-crystal datasets for Fe and Ni, with improvements of 34.4% and 57.6% respectively compared to prior work.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the Fe improvement and adds Ni results, broadening the empirical scope and providing more detail.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The VFZO framework facilitates lower interpolation error through the use of significantly more input data.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not explicitly state that lower error is due to using significantly more input data; that specific causal claim is not present in the listed publication claims.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The noise and non-uniform sampling in the Fe dataset complicate the resolution of low grain boundary energy (GBE) features, while the Ni dataset cusps are resolved with high accuracy.
alignments.rationale: This limitation/observation about dataset noise and sampling is present in the publication but not in the preprint claims, so it is an added claim.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The maximum dimension of an O h VFZ is estimated to be approximately 66.5° in the grain boundary octonion sense.
alignments.rationale: The dimensional estimate for the VFZ is included in the publication claims but was not present in the preprint, hence an added claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The anonymity of blockchain has accelerated the growth of illegal activities and criminal behaviors on cryptocurrency platforms. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The anonymity of blockchain has accelerated the growth of illegal activities and criminal behaviors on cryptocurrency platforms. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: The preprint and publication sentences are effectively identical in meaning and emphasis, so the claim is unchanged across versions.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Existing methods for identity inference in blockchain cannot balance scalability and end-to-end architecture, resulting in high computational consumption and weak feature representation. (certainty=high, type=limitation)
alignments.rationale: Wording and scope are the same between preprint and publication; the limitation is restated without substantive change.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method, I2BGNN, effectively transforms the identity inference task into a graph classification pattern and avoids computation in large-scale graphs. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: Our conversational recommendation system increases efficiency in finding the best choice compared to traditional systems. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The claim is verbatim between preprint and publication, indicating no change.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Users reported a higher confidence rating in their final choice when using our recommendation system. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: 75% of users preferred our recommendation system over the baseline system that did not incorporate user feedback. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Exact match of the quantitative user-preference result between versions.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed Axial-Generative Adversarial Network (Axial-GAN) synthesizes high-resolution visible images from low-resolution thermal images. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method shows significant improvements in image quality and face verification performance compared to state-of-the-art works. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed approach is more efficient than related state-of-the-art methods. (certainty=high, type=result)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The augmentation of the GAN framework with axial-attention layers improves the modeling of long-range dependencies. (certainty=medium, type=result)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: NIR-VIS masked face recognition is problematic due to the lack of masked face data and significant occlusion of facial parts by masks. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)

alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed heterogeneous training method maximizes the mutual information shared by face representations of two domains. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.rationale: The concluding claim is reproduced verbatim with no substantive change in meaning or confidence.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The Tensor Processing Primitives (TPP) provide a programming abstraction that enables efficient and portable implementation of deep learning workloads.
alignments.rationale: The contribution is stated identically in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The implementation of TPPs is highly optimized and platform-specific, while the TPP specification remains platform-agnostic.

alignments.rationale: The statement about implementation vs specification is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Standalone kernels and end-to-end deep learning and high-performance computing workloads expressed via TPPs outperform state-of-the-art implementations on multiple platforms.

alignments.rationale: The publication removes the explicit mention of high-performance computing workloads and emphasizes workloads "entirely" expressed via TPPs, narrowing the original broader performance claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We analytically prove that exponentially vanishing gradients, exponential cost concentration about the mean, and the exponential narrowness of minina occur together in parameterized quantum circuits.

alignments.rationale: The publication restates the analytical proof and the three linked phenomena with equivalent meaning and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: One can numerically diagnose barren plateaus via cost differences rather than via the computationally more expensive gradients.
alignments.pub_claim_text: One can numerically diagnose barren plateaus via cost differences rather than via the computationally more expensive gradients.
alignments.rationale: The diagnostic conclusion is reproduced exactly in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our work shows that quantum mechanics rules out certain cost landscapes that would otherwise be mathematically possible.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Quantum mechanics rules out certain cost landscapes that would otherwise be mathematically possible.
alignments.rationale: The publication drops the explicit "our work shows" framing, presenting the statement as a general conclusion rather than attributing it directly to the authors' work, which clarifies phrasing without changing scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed methodology for creating scientific news article representations utilizes a directed graph between scientific news articles and cited scientific publications.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The models were tested in the downstream task of link prediction for news article-paper links and news article-article topic links.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Agents trained with the behavior policy developed in this study are better equipped to handle collisions and risky randomized driver behavior.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Model-free learning agents trained with additional collision-prone scenario simulations demonstrate improved efficacy compared to regular simulation-based agents.
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert that agents trained with added collision-prone/risky scenarios perform better than standard simulation-trained agents; the publication preserves the claim intact.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of model-free learning agents trained with risk-prone driving scenarios is compared to baseline agents to assess improvement.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Model-free learning agents trained with additional collision-prone scenario simulations demonstrate improved efficacy compared to regular simulation-based agents.
alignments.rationale: The publication explicitly reports improved performance versus baseline agents, matching the preprint's comparative-performance claim without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Adding perturbations from risk-prone scenarios in the training process leads to measurable performance improvements.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The causal experimentation setup accounts for performance improvements across different driving scenarios by utilizing learning from risky situations.
alignments.rationale: The publication frames the improvement in terms of a causal experimentation setup that attributes gains to learning from risky situations, which clarifies the mechanism but does not reverse or remove the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed open set recognition method maximizes performance by setting strict decision boundaries that reject unknowns while maintaining satisfactory classification performance on knowns.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed open-set recognition method maximizes performance by setting strict decision boundaries that reject unknowns while maintaining satisfactory classification performance for knowns.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same contribution with essentially identical wording and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A novel network structure utilizing multiple one-vs-rest networks enhances the ability to reject nonmatches by learning class-specific discriminative features.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel network structure, which includes multiple one-vs-rest networks following a convolutional neural network feature extractor, assigns lower confidence scores to unknown samples compared to the softmax layer.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the novel multi one-vs-rest architecture but adds implementation detail (CNN feature extractor) and an explicit statement about lower confidence scores for unknowns, clarifying the mechanism.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The collective decision score, modeled by combining multiple decisions from the one-vs-rest networks, alleviates overgeneralization.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel network structure, which includes multiple one-vs-rest networks following a convolutional neural network feature extractor, assigns lower confidence scores to unknown samples compared to the softmax layer.
alignments.rationale: The publication's description of how the multi-network design reduces confidence for unknowns corresponds to the preprint's collective decision score mechanism but presents it with additional specifics, so it is a clarification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods in reducing overgeneralization based on extensive experiments conducted on various datasets.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods by effectively reducing overgeneralization.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same experimental outcome — significant outperformance in reducing overgeneralization — matching the preprint claim directly.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Machine learning methods can support self-adaptation by keeping runtime models up to date and reducing large adaptation spaces.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Machine learning methods can support self-adaptation by keeping runtime models up to date and reducing large adaptation spaces.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the preprint's contribution verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The application of machine learning methods may impact the decisions made by self-adaptive systems.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The application of machine learning methods may impact the decisions made by self-adaptive systems.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the same conclusion about ML impacting adaptation decisions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A theoretical bound on the impact of the machine learning method on the predictions made by the verifier is determined using computational learning theory.
alignments.pub_claim_text: A theoretical bound on the impact of the machine learning method on the predictions made by the verifier is determined using computational learning theory.
alignments.rationale: The publication reports the same theoretical result as the preprint with no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The study combines linear regression with statistical model checking to analyze adaptation options in a self-adaptive system.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The study combines linear regression with statistical model checking to analyze adaptation options in self-adaptive systems.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the methodological contribution unchanged.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Federated learning (FL) allows remarkable end-to-end energy savings of 30%-40% for wireless systems characterized by low bit/Joule efficiency.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Federated learning (FL) allows remarkable end-to-end energy savings of 30% to 40% in low-rate/power IoT communications.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the quantified energy savings but specifies the context as low-rate/power IoT communications (narrower than the more general 'low bit/Joule efficiency' wireless systems in the preprint).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Consensus-driven FL does not require the parameter server and further reduces emissions in mesh networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not include a corresponding claim about consensus-driven FL eliminating a parameter server or specifically reducing emissions in mesh networks, so this preprint claim appears to have been removed.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: All FL policies are slower to converge when local data are unevenly distributed, often 2x slower than centralized learning (CL).
alignments.pub_claim_text: FL is slower to converge when local data are unevenly distributed, often being 2x slower than centralized learning (CL).
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the convergence-slowdown result verbatim, preserving scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed analytical framework models both learning and communication energy costs, as well as carbon equivalent emissions.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed analytical framework evaluates both learning and communication energy costs, as well as the incurred greenhouse gas emissions.
alignments.rationale: The publication retains the analytical-framework contribution, with equivalent phrasing for energy costs and emissions (greenhouse gas/carbon equivalent).

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Neural-network states have strictly the same or higher expressive power than practically usable variational tensor networks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Neural-network states have strictly the same or higher expressive power than practically usable variational tensor networks.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the preprint's high-level conclusion verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: All matrix product states can be efficiently written as neural-network states with a number of edges polynomial in the bond dimension and depth logarithmic in the system size.
alignments.pub_claim_text: All matrix product states can be efficiently written as neural-network states with a number of edges polynomial in the bond dimension and depth that is logarithmic in the system size.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the technical result with equivalent wording and scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: There exist quantum states that are not efficiently expressible in terms of matrix product states or PEPS, but that are instead efficiently expressible with neural network states.
alignments.pub_claim_text: There exist quantum states that are not efficiently expressible in terms of matrix product states or projected entangled pair states but that are instead efficiently expressible with neural network states.
alignments.rationale: The publication maintains the preprint's conclusion about states efficiently expressible by neural-network states but not by tensor-network classes.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Applying dx-privacy to BERT fine-tuning in NLU applications demonstrates effective privacy preservation. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Applying dχ-privacy to BERT fine-tuning in NLU applications has privacy and utility implications. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same result (dχ/dx notation difference only) about applying the privacy mechanism to BERT with equivalent meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed privacy-adaptive LM pretraining methods can boost the utility of BERT dramatically while retaining the same level of privacy protection. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed privacy-adaptive LM pretraining methods can dramatically boost the utility of BERT while retaining the same level of privacy protection. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Text and meaning are effectively identical; the publication preserves the same strong utility-with-privacy claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The research quantifies the level of privacy preservation and provides guidance on privacy configuration. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The level of privacy preservation can be quantified, providing guidance on privacy configuration. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Both statements assert quantification of privacy and guidance for configuration with equivalent scope and confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The findings lay the groundwork for future explorations of privacy-preserving NLU with pretrained LMs. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The findings lay the groundwork for future explorations of privacy-preserving NLU with pretrained LMs. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The concluding claim is carried forward verbatim, preserving meaning and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HIVE-COTE has remained state of the art for accuracy on the UCR time series classification archive since its proposal in 2016. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HIVE-COTE has remained state of the art for accuracy on the UCR time series classification archive since its proposal in 2016. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim about HIVE-COTE's state-of-the-art status is preserved.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed changes to the HIVE-COTE algorithm significantly improve its accuracy and usability. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed changes to the HIVE-COTE algorithm significantly improve its accuracy and usability. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Publication retains the same claim of significant improvements in accuracy and usability.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: HIVE-COTE 2.0 is significantly more accurate than the current state of the art on 112 univariate UCR archive datasets and 26 multivariate UEA archive datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: HIVE-COTE 2.0 is significantly more accurate on average than the current state of the art on 112 univariate UCR archive datasets and 26 multivariate UEA archive datasets. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication adds "on average," clarifying the metric (average improvement) and narrowing the scope from an absolute claim to an average-effect claim while keeping confidence the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Two novel classifiers, the Temporal Dictionary Ensemble (TDE) and Diverse Representation Canonical Interval Forest (DrCIF), replace existing ensemble members in HIVE-COTE 2.0. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Two novel classifiers, the Temporal Dictionary Ensemble and Diverse Representation Canonical Interval Forest, replace existing ensemble members in HIVE-COTE 2.0. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication carries forward the same contribution describing the two novel classifiers.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Biomedical questions are more likely to require list-type answers than factoid-type answers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Biomedical questions are more likely to require list-type answers than factoid-type answers. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The result about answer type distribution in biomedical QA is unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose a sequence tagging approach for BioEQA that operates in a multi-span extraction setting. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a sequence tagging approach for BioEQA that operates in a multi-span extraction setting. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The proposed methodological contribution is preserved verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach can learn to decide the number of answers for a question from training data. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This specific claim about learning to decide answer count is not present in the publication's listed claims and appears omitted from the final paper.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our experimental results on the BioASQ 7b and 8b list-type questions outperformed the best-performing existing models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our experimental results on the BioASQ 7b and 8b list-type questions outperformed the best-performing existing models. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental outperforming result is carried forward identically.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FedSAE converges faster than FedAvg, the vanilla FL framework, in a highly heterogeneous system. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: FedSAE converges faster than FedAvg in a highly heterogeneous system. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The convergence claim is preserved with equivalent wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FedSAE improves test accuracy by 26.7% compared to FedAvg. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: FedSAE improves test accuracy by 26.7% compared to FedAvg. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Exact numerical improvement is retained in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FedSAE reduces stragglers by 90.3% on average. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: FedSAE reduces stragglers by 90.3% on average compared to FedAvg. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication clarifies the baseline for the straggler reduction (compared to FedAvg) but preserves the numerical claim and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: FedSAE self-adaptively adjusts the amount of training load per client in each round based on the reliability of each device. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: FedSAE self-adaptively adjusts the amount of training load per client in each round. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication keeps the self-adaptive load adjustment claim but omits the explicit "based on reliability" phrase, slightly rephrasing and clarifying the mechanism.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The paper suggests an extension of a Bayesian method for robust and interpretable feature construction and selection in the context of time series extrinsic regression (TSER). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed extension of a Bayesian method improves robust and interpretable feature construction and selection for time series extrinsic regression (TSER). (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication strengthens the wording from "suggests an extension" to asserting that the extension "improves" feature construction and selection, increasing claim strength.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed approach builds various and simple representations of time series stored in a relational data scheme. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The approach utilizes a relational data scheme to create various simple representations of time series data. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Meaning is preserved though wording is slightly rephrased; the representation and relational-scheme claim remains the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A propositionalisation technique is applied to build interpretable features from secondary tables to flatten the data. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A propositionalisation technique is applied to build interpretable features from secondary tables in the context of TSER. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication adds context (TSER) making the claim more explicit while retaining the original meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Experimental validation on various benchmark data sets demonstrates the benefits of the suggested approach. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Experimental validation on various benchmark data sets demonstrates the benefits of the suggested approach. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The experimental validation claim is preserved verbatim in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Potential biases can creep in at every step of the medical imaging research process. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Potential biases can creep in at every step of developing and assessing methods in medical image analysis. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same general claim but specifies 'developing and assessing methods in medical image analysis', narrowing and clarifying the original 'research process' phrasing while keeping confidence high.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Initiatives to counteract the problems in medical imaging research are already being started. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Ongoing efforts are being made to counteract the problems associated with biases in medical image analysis. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same idea but focuses explicitly on efforts targeting biases in medical image analysis, a narrower formulation of the preprint claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A broad range of recommendations is provided to address problems in medical imaging research in the future. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Recommendations are provided on how to further address the challenges in the field of computer analysis of medical images. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication narrows the scope to 'computer analysis of medical images' and reduces stated certainty from high to medium, so the claim is clarified and confidence tempered.

alignments.pub_claim_text: Systematic challenges, including data limitations and research incentives, are slowing down progress in the field of computer analysis of medical images. (certainty=high, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: This conclusion about systematic challenges does not have an obvious counterpart in the preprint and appears as a new publication claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed deep online correction (DOC) framework for monocular visual odometry achieves a relative transform error (RTE) of 2.0% on the KITTI Odometry benchmark for Seq. 09. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed deep online correction (DOC) framework for monocular visual odometry achieves a relative transform error (RTE) of 2.0% on the KITTI Odometry benchmark for Seq. 09. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the exact numerical result with identical phrasing and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DOC framework does not require gradient propagation for parameters of CNNs, saving computation resources during inference phases. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DOC framework does not require gradient propagation for parameters of convolutional neural networks, saving computation resources during inference phases. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Same technical claim and certainty; wording expanded 'CNNs' to 'convolutional neural networks' without changing meaning.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The DOC framework fully relies on deep learning frameworks and does not use complex back-end optimization modules. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The DOC framework fully relies on deep learning frameworks and does not use complex back-end optimization modules. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim and certainty in publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The performance of the DOC method is outstanding compared to traditional monocular visual odometry frameworks and is comparable to hybrid methods. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The performance of the DOC method is outstanding compared to traditional monocular visual odometry frameworks and is comparable to hybrid methods. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Direct match: publication preserves the comparative performance claim and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PRISM is a probabilistic simplex component analysis approach that effectively identifies the vertices of a data-circumscribing simplex. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PRISM is a probabilistic simplex component analysis approach that effectively identifies the vertices of a data-circumscribing simplex. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Exact match between preprint and publication on the method description and claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The inference model of PRISM is sound, as the vertices are provably identifiable under certain assumptions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The inference model of PRISM is sound, as the vertices are provably identifiable under certain assumptions. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical theoretical identifiability claim and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: PRISM can be effective in combating noise when the number of data points is large. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: PRISM can be effective in combating noise when the number of data points is large. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Same empirical/concluding statement preserved in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The variational inference scheme in PRISM resembles a matrix factorization problem with a special regularizer. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The variational inference scheme in PRISM resembles a matrix factorization problem with a special regularizer. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Direct match: the publication retains the same characterization of the variational inference scheme.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Numerical results demonstrate the potential of PRISM in various applications. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Numerical results demonstrate the potential of PRISM in various applications. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: The publication presents the same claim about numerical results and their implications.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed sensitivity metric better represents quantization sensitivity compared to existing approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed sensitivity metric better represents quantization sensitivity compared to existing approaches. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Publication repeats the same comparative claim and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The novel sensitivity metric considers the effect of quantization error on task loss and interaction with other layers. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The novel sensitivity metric considers the effect of quantization error on task loss and interaction with other layers. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Exact match in description and stated certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The labeled data generation methods developed are not dependent on a specific operation of the neural network. (certainty=medium, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The labeled data generation methods developed are not dependent on a specific operation of the neural network. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves the same methodological claim but raises the certainty from medium to high, hence an amplification of confidence.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The generated data are more feasible to be applied to mixed-precision quantization. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The generated data are more feasible to apply to mixed-precision quantization. (certainty=medium, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Same practical applicability claim retained with essentially identical wording and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: A framework is provided to generate attribution benchmark datasets for regression problems in geosciences with known ground truth. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: A framework is provided to generate attribution benchmark datasets for regression problems in geosciences with known ground truth. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.rationale: Publication restates the framework contribution verbatim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The generated benchmark dataset allows for the comparison of estimated heatmaps from different XAI methods to the ground truth. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The generated benchmark dataset allows for the comparison of estimated heatmaps from different XAI methods to the ground truth. (certainty=high, type=result)
alignments.rationale: Identical claim about benchmarking capability and certainty.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Attribution benchmarks are important for the objective assessment and accurate implementation of XAI methods in geosciences. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: Attribution benchmarks are important for the objective assessment and accurate implementation of XAI methods in geosciences. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Same conclusion preserved in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The use of XAI methods can increase model trust and assist in discovering new science in the geosciences. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The use of XAI methods can increase model trust and assist in discovering new science in the geosciences. (certainty=medium, type=conclusion)
alignments.rationale: Publication retains the same concluding statement regarding benefits of XAI methods.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The COVIDx-US dataset is the largest open-access fully-curated dataset of COVID-19 related ultrasound imaging data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The COVIDx-US dataset is the first and largest open-access fully-curated benchmark lung ultrasound imaging dataset.
alignments.rationale: The publication restates the same core contribution while adding 'first' and framing it as a benchmark dataset, which clarifies and slightly reframes the original claim without changing its substance.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: COVIDx-US consists of 150 lung ultrasound videos and 12,943 processed images from various patient categories.
alignments.rationale: The publication does not report the dataset image/video counts present in the preprint; this specific quantitative claim is omitted.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The dataset was specifically curated for the purpose of building and evaluating artificial intelligence algorithms and models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The dataset is reproducible, easy-to-use, and easy-to-scale due to its well-documented modular design.
alignments.rationale: The publication shifts emphasis from an explicit AI-curation purpose to dataset design and usability/reproducibility, revising the stated purpose and removing the explicit AI-focused framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Agents trained with our approach can take human-like trajectories in collision avoidance and goal-directed steering tasks.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Agents trained with our approach can take human-like trajectories in collision avoidance and goal-directed steering tasks.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats this result verbatim, indicating no substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our approach outperforms the experts as well as learning-based agents trained without knowledge distillation.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our approach outperforms the experts as well as learning-based agents trained without knowledge distillation.
alignments.rationale: The claim is carried over unchanged from preprint to publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We propose a framework for learning a human-like general collision avoidance policy for agent-agent interactions in fully decentralized, multi-agent environments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We propose a framework for learning a human-like general collision avoidance policy for agent-agent interactions in fully decentralized, multi-agent environments.
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed method reduces variability in treatment planning for low-dose-rate prostate brachytherapy by training a model on a large pool of successful retrospective data.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed method generates consistent treatment plans from a large pool of successful clinical data involving 961 patients.
alignments.rationale: The publication conveys the same idea of reducing variability/producing consistency and adds the concrete dataset size (961 patients), clarifying the original claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Performance analysis on 150 test cases demonstrates that the model produces results comparable to manual prehistorical plans.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Performance analysis on 150 test cases demonstrates comparable results to manual plans.
alignments.rationale: The performance result on 150 cases is restated with the same conclusion and dataset size.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The clinical target volume covering 100% of the prescribed dose was 98.9% for the proposed method compared to 99.4% for manual plans.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The clinical target volume covered by 100% of the prescribed dose was 98.9% for the proposed method compared to 99.4% for manual plans.
alignments.rationale: This numeric dosimetric comparison is carried forward exactly into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Using the proposed model, the planning time was significantly reduced to an average of 2.5 minutes per plan with simulated annealing and less than 3 seconds without it.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Using the proposed model, the planning time was significantly reduced to an average of 3 seconds per plan, compared to around 20 minutes for manual planning.
alignments.rationale: The publication drops the simulated-annealing branch and emphasizes the best-case ~3s timing with an explicit comparison to manual planning, changing the original timing presentation and strengthening the speed-up claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The UIT-ViCTSD dataset consists of 10,000 human-annotated comments for constructive and toxic speech detection.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The UIT-ViCTSD dataset consists of 10,000 human-annotated comments for constructive and toxic speech detection.
alignments.rationale: The dataset size and contribution are identical between preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed system using the PhoBERT model achieves F1-scores of 78.59% for classifying constructive comments and 59.40% for classifying toxic comments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed system using the PhoBERT model achieves F1-scores of 78.59% for classifying constructive comments and 59.40% for classifying toxic comments.
alignments.rationale: The reported performance metrics are unchanged in the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The implementation of various baseline models demonstrates the effectiveness of the dataset for evaluating constructive and toxic speech detection.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The implementation of various baseline models demonstrates the effectiveness of the dataset for evaluating traditional Machine Learning and Deep Neural Network-Based models.
alignments.rationale: The publication specifies the kinds of models evaluated (traditional ML and DNN), clarifying the original, more general claim about baselines.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The framework developed can automatically identify constructiveness and toxicity in Vietnamese social media comments.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The framework developed can automatically identify constructiveness and toxicity in Vietnamese social media comments.
alignments.rationale: This conclusion about the framework's capability is repeated without change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We devise a generic unsupervised technique to learn multimodal affinities between textual entities in a document-image.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We devise a generic unsupervised technique to learn multimodal affinities between textual entities in a document-image.
alignments.rationale: The contribution statement is identical in preprint and publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Our technique can operate on highly varying images spanning a wide range of documents.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Our technique can operate on highly varying images spanning a wide range of documents.
alignments.rationale: The capability claim is unchanged between versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: We demonstrate the applicability of our technique for various editing operations manipulating the content, appearance and geometry of the image.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate the applicability of our technique for various editing operations manipulating the content, appearance, and geometry of the image.
alignments.rationale: The demonstration claims are semantically identical; differences are only minor punctuation.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The Danish Fungi 2020 (DF20) dataset is unique due to its taxonomy-accurate class labels and rich observation metadata. (certainty=high, type=contribution)
alignments.pub_claim_text: The Danish Fungi 2020 (DF20) dataset is unique due to its taxonomy-accurate class labels and rich observation metadata. (certainty=high, type=contribution)

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The R package DoubleML implements the double/debiased machine learning framework of Chernozhukov et al. (2018).

alignments.rationale: Both statements describe the same contribution; the publication omits the specific citation but retains the same implementation claim, a minor wording clarification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: DoubleML enables valid inference in a variety of causal models, including partially linear and interactive regression models.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DoubleML enables inference in a variety of causal models, including partially linear and interactive regression models.
alignments.rationale: Both claims assert the package enables inference for the same class of causal models; wording is effectively equivalent.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The object-oriented implementation of DoubleML provides high flexibility for model specification and is easily extendable.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The object-oriented implementation of DoubleML allows for high flexibility in model specification.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Estimation of nuisance components in DoubleML can be performed using various state-of-the-art machine learning methods from the mlr3 ecosystem.
alignments.pub_claim_text: DoubleML users can perform valid inference based on machine learning methods.
alignments.rationale: The preprint specifically cites estimation via mlr3 methods, whereas the publication states a more general capability to perform inference using machine learning methods, shifting and broadening the focus.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The representation of LiDAR readings is a key factor behind the degradation of agents' performance in unfamiliar scenarios.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The representation of LiDAR readings is a key factor behind the degradation of agents' performance in unfamiliar scenarios.
alignments.rationale: The publication repeats the same conclusion about LiDAR representation and performance degradation without substantive change.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The input pre-processing approach IPAPRec/IPAPRecN can highlight important short-distance values and compress the range of less-important long-distance values in laser scans.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The input preprocessing approach IPAPRec/IPAPRecN can highlight important short-distance values and compress the range of less-important long-distance values in laser scans.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: IPAPRec/IPAPRecN substantially improves navigation agents' generalization performance and greatly reduces the training time compared to conventional methods.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The methods IPAPRec/IPAPRecN substantially improve navigation agents' generalization performance and greatly reduce the training time compared to conventional methods.
alignments.rationale: The performance and training-time improvement claims are carried over verbatim into the publication.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The proposed polarization guided model can accurately separate specular reflection from diffuse components in images.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The proposed polarization guided model can accurately separate specular reflection from diffuse components in images.
alignments.rationale: The core result about accurate separation is identical in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The polarization chromaticity image generated by the model reveals the geometrical profile of the input image in complex illumination scenarios.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The polarization chromaticity image generated by the model can effectively reveal the geometrical profile of the input image under diverse illumination conditions.
alignments.rationale: The publication slightly rephrases the claim to emphasize effectiveness under diverse illumination, a clarification of the original wording without changing scope.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The method reformulates specular reflection separation into a unified optimization function solvable by the ADMM strategy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: The method reformulates specular reflection separation into a unified optimization function solvable by the ADMM strategy.
alignments.rationale: The methodological formulation and solution approach are stated the same way in both versions.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: Qualitative and quantitative experimental results demonstrate that the method can effectively separate specular reflection in challenging scenarios.
alignments.pub_claim_text: Qualitative and quantitative experimental results demonstrate that the method can faithfully separate specular reflection in challenging scenarios.
alignments.rationale: Both assert experimental support for effective/faithful separation in challenging scenarios; wording is slightly adjusted but the claim is the same.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: d3p is a software package designed for efficient Bayesian inference under differential privacy guarantees.
alignments.pub_claim_text: d3p is a software package designed to help fielding runtime efficient widely-applicable Bayesian inference under differential privacy guarantees.
alignments.rationale: The publication expands on the original by emphasizing runtime efficiency and wide applicability, a clarification/expansion of the original contribution claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: d3p allows users to fit any parametric probabilistic model with a differentiable density function.
alignments.pub_claim_text: d3p achieves general applicability to a wide range of probabilistic modelling problems by implementing the differentially private variational inference algorithm.
alignments.rationale: The preprint's claim about fitting 'any parametric' differentiable-density model is reframed in the publication as general applicability through a specific DPVI algorithm, changing the emphasis and technical framing.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: The software demonstrates a $\text{10}$ fold speed-up in runtime for private inference compared to an implementation using TensorFlow Privacy.
alignments.pub_claim_text: We perform an empirical evaluation of the runtime of the private inference on a complex model and find a ~10 fold speed-up compared to an implementation using TensorFlow Privacy.
alignments.rationale: Both versions report the same empirical ~10x runtime speed-up relative to a TensorFlow Privacy implementation.

alignments.pub_claim_text: We demonstrate the use of our software on a hierarchical logistic regression example, showing the expressiveness of the modelling approach as well as the ease of running the parameter inference.
alignments.rationale: The publication includes an example demonstration (hierarchical logistic regression) not stated in the preprint, representing an added illustrative claim.

alignments.preprint_claim_text: SuSketch provides gameplay predictions for two competing players of specific character classes.
alignments.pub_claim_text: SuSketch provides gameplay predictions for two competing players of specific character classes.

alignments.pub_claim_text: The system proactively designs alternatives to the level and class pairing, improving the predicted balance of the game.
alignments.rationale: The publication preserves this system capability claim without substantive changes.

alignments.pub_claim_text: #PraCegoVer encourages more works addressing the automatic generation of descriptions in Portuguese.
